name: HITEN MITSURUGI RYU NO HIKEN: AMA KAKERU RYU NO HIRAMEKI (HITEN MITSURUGI STYLE SUCCESSION TECHNIQUE: HEAVEN'S SOARING DRAGON FLASH)

description.value:
Type: Taijutsu (Strike)

Rank: 9

Chakra Cost: 18

Learn DC: 27 | Successes: 6

Perform: 14 | Perform DC: 34

Components: C, F, Mas, Mob

This ultimate technique of the Hiten Mitsurugi style is the reason why there is only one master and one student at any time, as the technique is passed on only in a fight to the death between master and student. Should the student live, they are the new master. Should the student fail, they die, and the master will find a new student to teach.

You muse be moving at Speed Rank 4 or higher, and your weapon must be sheathed in a solid sheath. Make an attack against the target, which must make a Fortitude save. If the target fails, it is slain immediately. If the target succeeds, it suffers 8d6 damage of a type appropriate for your weapon, and an additional 5d6 damage of the same type on its next turn (Fortitude halves the additional damage). If the attack misses, the target loses its Dexterity bonus to Defense (Reflex negates), and you make a second attack at a -5 penalty, whose effects are as the first attack, but the save DC is decreased by 5, and the damage suffered on a successful save is reduced to 6d6, plus 4d6 additional damage the next turn.

You become @Condition[fatigued]{fatigued} once this technique has finished, and your katana will be unsheathed and held in hand. If used while fatigued, you take a -4 penalty to meet the Perform Requirements. If used while exhausted, you take a -8 penalty to meet the Perform Requirements, fall unconscious for 1d4 hours after using the technique, and will be exhausted once you wake.

If you fail to meet the Perform Requirements when attempting to perform this technique, you provoke attacks of opportunity, fall @Condition[prone]{prone}, and lose your Dexterity bonus to Defense until your next turn.

This technique's Rank counts as 12 for the purpose of its Learn DC and identifying it.

Mastery: Each step reduces the attack penalty to the second attack by -1, and increases the save DC of the second attack by 1 (thus improving it to be identical to the first attack at the fifth step).
